rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #8, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/02/2020. 1) Notice must be given after a pleading requiring notice is filed. The proof of service shows notice was given 5 days before this petition was filed. See LRSF 14.21A. 2) The date of hearing set forth in the notice is incorrect. 3) The attorney's statement regarding the retention of the original bank statement must in the form of a declaration signed by the attorney. LRSF 14.93D. 4) A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: A) The current amount of bond and sufficiency must be alleged. B) Is the current beneficiary's suite number accurate? Previous filings show a different suite number. C) When did Patricia Krebbs' address change? D) Are the disbursements described as "travel, food, and trip to Attorneys office" proper expenses of administration of this trust? E) Paragraph 8 of the petition refers to "Seventh Account". Did the petitioner mean "Eighth Account"? F) More specific descriptions of services by the attorney for the "preparation of the account" are needed, together with a breakdown of time spent on each type of service. Did the attorney spend 5 hours drafting this petition? This issue can be addressed by the attorney's declaration.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHARLES THOMAS ELLIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Approval Of Assets Distribution After Issuance Of Order For Final Distribution; Final Discharge; Discharge Of Personal Representative; Discharge Of Surety
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/2/20. (No earlier available date.) If the balance on hand as of 10/7/19 was $208,169.96, after payment of the fees ordered on 10/7/19 ($23,600 to the attorney and $15,000 to the administrator), the net available for distribution should have been $56,523.32 to each of the three heirs, from which share each of Eric Ringer and Griffin Ellis was to reimburse Danielle Ellis $12,808.06. The $25,616.12 that Eric Ringer and Griffin Ellis owed to Danielle Ellis, however, was paid out of the estate, resulting in a distribution to Danielle Ellis of only $47,981.28 and increasing the distributions to Eric Ringer and Griffin Ellis. Either her written consent must be filed or an appearance will be required on 11/2/20 for the Court to determine if the action should be approved. The math shown in paragraph 6 of this petition appears to be incorrect regarding the total distributions (and disbursements) shown on page 2 of the petition.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. remote appearance highly encouraged. Please review the 6th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. ISSUES (to be addressed at hearing): 1) On the attorney timesheets entry 03/06/2020 Reviewed continued hearing date to 04/13/2020 due to examiner illness. Examiner did not continue the matter due to examiner illness. The IJS entry states continued to 04/13/2020. Examiner notes emailed 03/05/2020. 2) Petitioner is requesting $2,668.00 for services as conservator of person and $14,297.00 in fees for conservator of the estate. Timesheets allege $11,310.00 in fees as conservator of the estate. 3) The court does not allow fees for routine research. Examiner is unable to determine how much time was spent for research on each entry. The following entries for Conservator of the Estate showing research. 03/13/2019, 03/15/2019, 03/21/2019, 3/27/2019, 3/29/2019, 04/01/2019, 04/05/2019, 04/12/2019, 04/15/2019, 04/17/2019, 04/18/2019, 04/19/2019, 04/24/2019, 04/25/2019, 05/13/2019, 06/05/2019, 06/25/2019, 06/28/2019, 07/01/2019, 07/15/2019, 07/24/2019, 07/30/2019, 08/02/2019, 08/12/2019, 09/05/2019, 09/10/2019, 09/19/2019. 4) Petitioner originally requested $9,720.00 for legal services and $171.35 in costs. With the 07/10/2020 Amendment Petitioner is now requesting $18,057.50 in fees and $204.10 in costs. 5) Petitioner is requesting conservator receive $145.00 per hour up to $1,450.00 without further court confirmation. 6) Petitioner is requesting additional attorney fees of $400.00 per hour up to $4,000.00 to Kato Feder & Suzuki to be paid without further court confirmation.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE 2007 HORST J. ALLENDORFER REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/02/2020. 1) Notice of hearing must be on a mandatory Judicial Council form DE-115. 2) Notice must include the Bank of America account as an asset subject to the petition. 3) Notice indicates the petitioner's capacity as co-trustee, even though the petitioner alleges it is acting as sole trustee. 4) An amendment including copies of all exhibits and allegations of the original petition sought to be considered by the Court must be filed and served, as the original petition was superseded by the amended petition.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WONG FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Re: 1) Removal Of Trustee; 2) Recovery Of Trust Property; 3) Surcharge Against Trustee And Fiduciary; 4)Production Of Accountings;
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/28/2020. Current Issues: Petition has not been reviewed. 1. File stamped single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court no later than 10/13/2020 at 4pm. (LRSF 2.7, 14.6). 2. A hard copy of the proposed order must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court no later than 10/13/2020 at 4pm. (LRSF 14.93). [Proposed orders may not be e-filed.] 3. Needs to file notice.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/26/20. (No earlier available date.) Courtesy copies are ENDORSED FILED copies. Examiners will not review papers until they have been filed. [LRSF 14.6 D.] Item 8 of the petition must include the person named in item 2 (the petitioner) and all alternative executors, who must also receive notice [Probate Code Section 8110(b)], and must clearly identify who the trustee of the devisee trust is. If the trustee is the same person as the proposed executor, the beneficiaries must all be listed, clearly identified as such, and must receive notice. [LRSF 14.26 D.2. and Probate Code Section 1208(b).]
